content:
Character: Elliot Park
Perception: Elliot is unassuming — slim, glasses, quiet — the sort of person you'd underestimate until you watch him debug a distributed system in twenty minutes. We met during a Wi-Fi disaster and ended up reconfiguring the building router together; I knew within an hour he was the real thing. Thursday evenings we drink beer and work in parallel — sometimes we talk about code, sometimes about nothing, and both feel fine. He's one of the few people whose silence I don't find suspicious. I think he's smarter than his job lets him be and lonelier than he'd admit. I know he built a bookshelf and it leans slightly left. I respect him for not fixing it.
summary:

Perception: Elliot is unassuming — slim, glasses, quiet — the sort of person you'd underestimate until you watch him debug a distributed system in twenty minutes. We met during a Wi-Fi disaster and ended up reconfiguring the building router together; I knew within an hour he was the real thing. Thursday evenings we drink beer and work in parallel — sometimes we talk about code, sometimes about nothing, and both feel fine. He's one of the few people whose silence I don't find suspicious. I think he's smarter than his job lets him be and lonelier than he'd admit. I know he built a bookshelf and it leans slightly left. I respect him for not fixing it.
